verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Afternoon — 16:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: Rey Cortiço at Afternoon — 16:00 looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the weak source agreement could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 60 kJ, 9 s period, lully 30/100, and wind is not fully clear.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a small window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 37 kJ, 9 s period, lully 14/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.
warnings: 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 39 kJ, 9 s period, lully 26/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Meio da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size; Meio da Baia may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Meio da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size
Meio da Baia may feel too soft without more push

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 43 kJ, 9 s period, lully 27/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
